title_en: Types of Ilal and Their Identification
content_en:
Al-Ilal of ad-Daraqutni provides extensive documentation of the different types of hidden defects that can affect hadith reliability, illustrated through his analysis of specific cases. Understanding these types and how they are identified requires understanding the dynamics of oral transmission across multiple generations — the environment in which these errors originated.

The most common type of illah is the confusion of the marfu' with the mawquf — transmitting a hadith as attributed to the Prophet when it is actually a statement of a Companion or Follower that has been inadvertently elevated through transmission error. This happens when a narrator who heard both a prophetic hadith and a similar Companion statement confuses the two and attributes the Companion's statement to the Prophet in later transmission. Identifying this error requires knowing both the prophetic hadith and the Companion statement separately, recognizing their similarity, and determining which was the original source in the chain under examination.

Another common illah is the reversal of narrator direction in a chain — where a hadith that was actually transmitted from B to A is mistakenly recorded as transmitted from A to B. This typically happens when a narrator who transmitted in both directions — who received from B and also transmitted to B — is confused in the chain. Identifying this error requires knowing which direction the transmission actually ran, which requires biographical knowledge of the dates and teaching relationships of the narrators involved.

The insertion of an additional narrator where none exists — known as ziyadah thiqa (addition by a reliable narrator) — is another problematic defect. A reliable narrator who is also scrupulously accurate in their transmission might on one occasion incorrectly insert an additional name into a chain, creating the appearance of a longer chain than actually existed. This type of error is particularly difficult to identify because it involves a reliable narrator and the defect is in a direction of greater rather than lesser detail.

Ad-Daraqutni's analysis in al-Ilal moves through these and other types with extraordinary precision. His judgments about specific hadith — which variant is sound, where the defect lies, and what type of error it represents — demonstrate a quality of critical analysis that subsequent scholars recognized as setting the standard for the field.

The practical implications of ilal criticism for Islamic jurisprudence are significant. A hadith that appears sound on a surface examination of its chain — all narrators reliable, chain continuous — but that has an illah may not be reliable as legal evidence. The identification and documentation of ilal thus directly affects how Islamic law is derived from hadith sources.
content_id:
Al-'Ilal karya ad-Daraqutni menyediakan dokumentasi yang luas tentang berbagai jenis cacat tersembunyi yang dapat mempengaruhi keandalan hadis, yang diilustrasikan melalui analisisnya terhadap kasus-kasus tertentu. Memahami jenis-jenis ini dan bagaimana mengidentifikasinya mengharuskan pemahaman tentang dinamika periwayatan lisan lintas beberapa generasi — lingkungan di mana kesalahan-kesalahan ini bermula.

Jenis 'illah yang paling umum adalah kerancuan antara marfu' dan mawquf — meriwayatkan sebuah hadis sebagai dinisbatkan kepada Nabi, padahal itu sebenarnya adalah perkataan Sahabat atau Tabi'in yang secara tidak sengaja diangkat melalui kesalahan periwayatan. Ini terjadi ketika seorang perawi yang mendengar baik hadis kenabian maupun perkataan Sahabat yang serupa mencampuradukkan keduanya dan menyandarkan perkataan Sahabat kepada Nabi dalam periwayatan selanjutnya. Mengidentifikasi kesalahan ini mengharuskan pengetahuan terpisah tentang hadis kenabian maupun perkataan Sahabat, mengenali kemiripan keduanya, dan menentukan mana yang merupakan sumber asli dalam sanad yang sedang diperiksa.

'Illah umum lainnya adalah pembalikan arah perawi dalam suatu sanad — di mana hadis yang sebenarnya diriwayatkan dari B kepada A secara keliru dicatat sebagai diriwayatkan dari A kepada B. Ini biasanya terjadi ketika seorang perawi yang meriwayatkan dalam dua arah — yang menerima dari B dan juga meriwayatkan kepada B — mengalami kerancuan dalam sanad. Mengidentifikasi kesalahan ini mengharuskan pengetahuan tentang arah periwayatan yang sebenarnya berjalan, yang memerlukan pengetahuan biografis tentang tanggal dan hubungan pengajaran para perawi yang terlibat.

Penyisipan perawi tambahan yang tidak ada — yang dikenal sebagai ziyadah tsiqah (penambahan oleh perawi terpercaya) — adalah 'illah problematik lainnya. Seorang perawi terpercaya yang juga sangat teliti dalam periwayatannya mungkin pada suatu kesempatan secara keliru menyisipkan nama tambahan ke dalam sanad, menciptakan kesan sanad yang lebih panjang dari yang sebenarnya ada. Jenis kesalahan ini sangat sulit diidentifikasi karena melibatkan perawi terpercaya dan cacatnya berada dalam arah yang lebih rinci, bukan lebih sederhana.

Analisis ad-Daraqutni dalam al-'Ilal menelusuri jenis-jenis ini dan jenis-jenis lainnya dengan ketepatan yang luar biasa. Penilaiannya tentang hadis-hadis tertentu — varian mana yang sahih, di mana letak cacatnya, dan jenis kesalahan apa yang diwakilinya — memperlihatkan kualitas analisis kritis yang diakui oleh para ulama sesudahnya sebagai standar tertinggi di bidang ini.

Implikasi praktis dari kritik 'ilal bagi fikih Islam sangat signifikan. Sebuah hadis yang tampak sahih pada pemeriksaan permukaan sanadnya — semua perawi terpercaya, sanad bersambung — namun memiliki 'illah mungkin tidak dapat diandalkan sebagai dalil hukum. Identifikasi dan dokumentasi 'ilal dengan demikian secara langsung mempengaruhi bagaimana hukum Islam diturunkan dari sumber-sumber hadis.
content_ar: Al-Ilal of ad-Daraqutni provides extensive documentation of the different types of hidden defects that can affect الحديث reliability, illustrated through his analysis of specific cases. الفهم these types and how they are identified requires الفهم the dynamics of oral transmission across multiple generations — the environment in which these errors originated. The most common type of illah is the confusion of the marfu' with the mawquf — transmitting a الحديث as attributed to النبي ﷺ عندما it is actually a statement of a Companion or Follower that has been inadvertently elevated through transmission error. This happens عندما a narrator who heard both a prophetic الحديث and a similar Companion statement confuses the two and attributes the Companion's statement to النبي ﷺ in later transmission. Identifying this error requires knowing both the prophetic الحديث and the Companion statement separately, recognizing their similarity, and determining which was the original source in the chain under examination. Another common illah is the reversal of narrator direction in a chain — where a الحديث that was actually transmitted from B to A is mistakenly recorded as transmitted from A to B. This typically happens عندما a narrator who transmitted in both directions — who received from B and أيضاً transmitted to B — is confused in the chain. Identifying this error requires knowing which direction the transmission actually ran, which requires biographical العلم of the dates and teaching relationships of the narrators involved. The insertion of an additional narrator where none exists — known as ziyadah thiqa (addition by a reliable narrator) — is another problematic defect. A reliable narrator who is أيضاً scrupulously accurate in their transmission might on one occasion incorrectly insert an additional name into a chain, creating the appearance of a longer chain than actually existed. This type of error is particularly difficult to identify لأن it involves a reliable narrator and the defect is in a direction of greater rather than lesser detail. Ad-Daraqutni's analysis in al-Ilal moves through these and other types with extraordinary precision. His judgments about specific الحديث — which variant is sound, where the defect lies, and what type of error it represents — demonstrate a quality of البالغ الأهمية analysis that subsequent العلماء recognized as setting the standard for the field. The practical implications of ilal criticism for Islamic jurisprudence are المهم. A الحديث that appears sound on a surface examination of its chain — all narrators reliable, chain continuous — but that has an illah may not be reliable as legal evidence. The identification and documentation of ilal وهكذا directly affects how Islamic law is derived from الحديث sources.
